---
title: "Sports Analytics"
page-layout: full
---

```{=html}
<div class="container" style="padding: 3rem 0; max-width: 900px;">

  <p class="section-label">Sports Analytics</p>

  <p style="max-width: 660px; color: var(--text-muted);">
    The same statistical rigour I apply to concept drift and non-stationary data streams 
    translates naturally to sports. Here I explore performance metrics, player analytics, 
    and predictive modelling in football, basketball, and beyond.
  </p>

  <!-- Coming soon / placeholder projects -->
  <div class="grid-2" style="margin-top: 2.5rem;">
    <div class="pub-card">
      <span class="tag">Football</span>
      <span class="tag">Python</span>
      <h4 style="margin-top: 0.8rem;">Expected Goals (xG) Modelling</h4>
      <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.9rem;">
        Building xG models from shot event data using logistic regression and gradient 
        boosting — quantifying shot quality beyond simple goal counts.
      </p>
      <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.8rem; font-style: italic; margin-top: 0.8rem;">
        📁 Coming soon on GitHub
      </p>
    </div>

    <div class="pub-card">
      <span class="tag">Basketball</span>
      <span class="tag">R</span>
      <h4 style="margin-top: 0.8rem;">Player Efficiency Under Pressure</h4>
      <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.9rem;">
        Analysing how player performance metrics shift in high-leverage game situations — 
        a sports application of non-stationarity and distribution drift.
      </p>
      <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.8rem; font-style: italic; margin-top: 0.8rem;">
        📁 Coming soon on GitHub
      </p>
    </div>

    <div class="pub-card">
      <span class="tag">Football</span>
      <span class="tag">Machine Learning</span>
      <h4 style="margin-top: 0.8rem;">Transfer Value Prediction</h4>
      <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.9rem;">
        Predicting player transfer market values using performance data, positional 
        metrics, and age-progression curves.
      </p>
      <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.8rem; font-style: italic; margin-top: 0.8rem;">
        📁 Coming soon on GitHub
      </p>
    </div>

    <div class="pub-card">
      <span class="tag">Multi-Sport</span>
      <span class="tag">Stats</span>
      <h4 style="margin-top: 0.8rem;">Drift in Team Performance</h4>
      <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.9rem;">
        Applying concept drift detection algorithms to time series of team performance 
        indicators across a season — when does a team fundamentally change?
      </p>
      <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.8rem; font-style: italic; margin-top: 0.8rem;">
        📁 Research in progress
      </p>
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- Blog posts placeholder -->
  <div style="margin-top: 4rem;">
    <p class="section-label">Analysis Posts</p>
    <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.9rem;">
      Detailed analyses and tutorials will be posted here. Topics will include:
    </p>
    <ul style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 2;">
      <li>How to build a shot map in Python with <code>mplsoccer</code></li>
      <li>Applying the KS-test to detect structural breaks in team form</li>
      <li>Clustering playing styles with k-means on event data</li>
      <li>A Ghanaian Premier League data analysis</li>
    </ul>
    <p style="color: var(--text-muted); font-size: 0.85rem; font-style: italic;">
      Subscribe via RSS or check back soon.
    </p>
  </div>

  <!-- Data sources -->
  <div style="margin-top: 4rem;">
    <p class="section-label">Data Sources & Tools I Use</p>
    <div style="display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.4rem;">
      <span class="tag">StatsBomb Open Data</span>
      <span class="tag">FBref</span>
      <span class="tag">Understat</span>
      <span class="tag">Basketball-Reference</span>
      <span class="tag">mplsoccer</span>
      <span class="tag">pandas</span>
      <span class="tag">scikit-learn</span>
      <span class="tag">matplotlib</span>
      <span class="tag">ggplot2</span>
      <span class="tag">tidyverse</span>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div style="margin-top: 3rem; text-align: center;">
    <a class="btn-outline-gold" href="https://github.com/Kuukudadzie" target="_blank">See My GitHub for Code →</a>
  </div>

</div>
```
